samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="state0">Initial state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state1">Detecting rising edge.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state2">Detecting falling edge.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic">State register.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="sequential_logic">Next state register.</reg>
        <reg id="rise_reg" type="sequential_logic">Rising edge register.</reg>
        <reg id="down_reg" type="sequential_logic">Falling edge register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
        <sequential>
            On the negative edge of rst_n, the state register is initialized to state0, the rising edge register rise_reg is set to 0, and the falling edge register down_reg is also set to 0.
            <statement>
                if (!rst_n) begin
                    state <= state0;
                    rise_reg <= 0;
                    down_reg <= 0;
                end
            </statement>
            When the clock signal clk has a positive edge, the state machine transitions based on the current state and input signal a.
            <statement>
                case (state)
            </statement>
            In state0, if the input signal a is 0, the state transitions to state1 to detect rising edges. If a is 1, the state remains the same.
            <statement>
                if (a == 0) begin
                    state <= state1;
                    rise_reg <= 0;
                end
                else begin
                    state <= state0;
                    rise_reg <= 0;
                end
            </statement>
            In state1, if a is 1, the state transitions to state2 to detect falling edges. If a is 0, the state remains the same.
            <statement>
                if (a == 1) begin
                    state <= state2;
                    rise_reg <= 1;
                end
                else begin
                    state <= state1;
                    rise_reg <= 0;
                end
            </statement>
            In state2, if a is 0, the state transitions back to state0. If a is 1, the state remains the same.
            <statement>
                if (a == 0) begin
                    state <= state0;
                    down_reg <= 0;
                end
                else begin
                    state <= state2;
                    down_reg <= 0;
                end
            </statement>
            The rise output signal is set to 1 when a rising edge is detected, and it is set to 0 otherwise. The down output signal is set to 1 when a falling edge is detected, and it is set to 0 otherwise.
            <statement>
                rise <= rise_reg;
                down <= down_reg;
            </statement>
        </sequential>
    </implementation>
</module>
